summary.implementation_agent: backend-impl (finished manually by operator after run1–run4 failed)

findings.title: KNOWN_SPEC_DRIFT is empty — spec was fully updated in-CR
findings.description: S01 chose to fix doc drift directly in docs/IW_AI_Core_CLI_Spec.md §4 rather than add allowlist entries. ~30 missing commands were added to §4. KNOWN_SPEC_DRIFT has 0 entries. This is the preferred outcome per the CR design: 'prefer fixing the doc over allowlisting'. The spec document is now fully in sync with the live CLI (62=62 bidirectional).
findings.evidence: KNOWN_SPEC_DRIFT: {} (0 entries) confirmed by Python import of test_cli_spec_conformance

findings.title: KNOWN_UNTESTED_COMMANDS baseline: 57 entries — follow-up must shrink this
findings.description: The conformance test's assertion 3 (coverage check) is pre-seeded with 57 entries covering every non-priority command. This is expected and correct for a first-merge. The allowlist makes the gate a ratchet: it will fire the moment a newly added command ships without a contract test. CR-00073 covers 5 priority commands with dedicated *_contract.py test files (step-done, register, doc-update, approve, next-id) plus evidence-ingestion hooks. The 57-entry baseline is the workload for the follow-up coverage extension work.
findings.evidence: KNOWN_UNTESTED_COMMANDS: 57 entries confirmed by Python import
findings.follow_up: TESTS_ENHANCEMENT item 3.3 follow-up: shrink KNOWN_UNTESTED_COMMANDS by adding contract tests for non-priority commands

findings.title: Contract test counts: 44 tests across 6 files (43 passed, 1 xfailed)
findings.description: The per-command contract tests delivered: test_step_done_contract.py (9 tests), test_register_contract.py (11 tests), test_doc_update_contract.py (6 tests), test_approve_contract.py (7 tests), test_next_id_contract.py (7 tests), test_evidence_hooks_contract.py (4 tests). Total: 44 contract tests. 43 pass + 1 strict xfail (test_doc_update_new_doc_without_tier_is_clean_usage_error). The xfail pins a genuine CLI bug (TypeError in DocService.create_doc when --tier is missing) rather than test flakiness.
findings.evidence: S01 report: '43 passed + 1 xfailed'; S09 integration-tests gate: 3031 passed (includes CLI contract tests)
findings.follow_up: File I-00NN for doc-update missing-tier TypeError exit-3 (operator follow-up; already noted in S01 report)

findings.title: next-id concurrency test (ThreadPoolExecutor) is stable
findings.description: test_next_id_increments_sequence_row uses ThreadPoolExecutor to issue 10 concurrent next-id calls and verifies no duplicate IDs. S01 originally had an off-by-one bug in the assertion (asserted next_number == prior+1, but next_number holds the NEXT value to hand out). The operator fixed it. The corrected test passed under pytest-randomly with the default seed. No special isolation (beyond testcontainer DB) was required.
findings.evidence: S01 report: 'test_next_id_increments_sequence_row off-by-one fixed'; S09 passed

findings.title: S01 automated runs (run1–run4) all failed; operator completed manually
findings.description: The four automated agent runs for S01 all failed without reporting: run1 crashed early (PID dead), run2 and run4 hit the 2400s timeout. The operator diagnosed and fixed latent defects in the partially-written test suite: (1) subprocess-test deadlock from duplicate PK in test_project fixture, (2) live-DB-guard / orch-DB resolution via IW_CORE_ORCH_DB_* pinning in iw_subprocess fixture, (3) next-id off-by-one assertion, (4) doc-update error test duplicates and misnamed cases, (5) assertion-scanner tautology in step-done bad-report-path test, (6) stray nested directory removal.
findings.evidence: S01 report: 'Step finished manually after run1-run4 failed'

findings.title: Scope discipline maintained: no orch/ production code edited
findings.description: git diff origin/main -- orch/ dashboard/ executor/ scripts/ is empty. All changes are in allowed_paths: tests/integration/cli/**, tests/integration/test_cli_spec_conformance.py, Makefile, docs/IW_AI_Core_CLI_Spec.md, docs/IW_AI_Core_Testing_Strategy.md, skills/iw-ai-core-testing/**, ai-dev/work/TESTS_ENHANCEMENT.md. The monkeypatch demonstration for TDD RED evidence was entirely in test code (throwaway file, run, output captured, file deleted).
findings.evidence: S01 report: 'git diff origin/main touches no production file'

findings.title: Manifest scope gap: spec-conformance file not in allowed_paths
findings.description: The workflow-manifest.json's scope.allowed_paths lists 'tests/integration/test_cli_core.py' etc. but does not include tests/integration/test_cli_spec_conformance.py, which is a required deliverable (AC2). This was already noted as an operator follow-up in the S01 report. The merge scope gate may have flagged it.
findings.evidence: workflow-manifest.json scope.allowed_paths vs CR-00073_CR_Design.md file manifest
findings.follow_up: Update manifest scope to include tests/integration/test_cli_spec_conformance.py for future CRs

findings.title: Genuine CLI bug surfaced by contract test — incident not yet filed
findings.description: test_doc_update_new_doc_without_tier_is_clean_usage_error is a strict xfail pinning a genuine CLI bug: doc-update creates a new doc and omits --tier/--editorial-category, crashing with raw TypeError from DocService.create_doc() surfaced as exit 3 Database error instead of a clean exit 2 usage error. The S01 report said 'operator to file an Incident' but no I-NNNNN was recorded in the report. This is a CLI bug, not a test bug, and is out of scope for CR-00073 (test-only CR).
findings.follow_up: File an Incident for doc-update missing-tier producing exit-3 TypeError instead of exit-2 usage error

test_summary: skipped: no tests for analysis step (self-assess is a process-improvement step, not a code-test step)
notes: Analysis draws on: S01 backend report, S04-S10 QvGate reports, workflow-manifest.json, test_cli_spec_conformance.py (imported to confirm allowlist sizes). S11 fix cycle (security-secrets) required 1 operator fix cycle. All 8 QV gates ultimately passed. No production code touched.
